chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-1702 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 23.10.2025 Mr. Sarbaz Ayaz Palijo advocate a/w petitioner Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri Assistant A.G Sindh a/w SIP Dileejan Rind of P.S Qasimabad and SIP Raza Muhammad Soomro of Police Checkpost Nasim Nagar Mr. Shahid Ahmed Shaikh, Additional P.G Sindh *** Learned Assistant A.G files report on behalf of Incharge Police Check Post Nasim Nagar Hyderabad, which is taken on record. He submits that impugned FIRs have been challaned and now the trial is pending before learned Judicial Magistrate-II Hyderabad, therefore, in view of the dicta laid down by the Supreme Court in the case of Director General Anti-Corruption Establishment Lahore and others vs. Muhammad Akram Khan and others [PLD 2013 SC 401] this petition has become infructuous and seeks its disposal accordingly. On being confronted with the aforesaid legal position, learned counsel for the petitioners also seeks disposal of the petition. In view of the above, this petition has become infructuous and the same stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar

In view of the above, this petition has become infructuous and the same stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-1811 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 23.10.2025 Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri Assistant A.G Sindh a/w ASI Nazeer Ahmed Lashari of P.S B-Section Nawabshah Mr. Shahid Ahmed Shaikh, Additional P.G Sindh *** Mr. Muzamil Khan advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of the petitioners, whereas Mr. Arshad Ali Keerio advocate files Vakalatnama of Mr. Parwaiz Ahmed Pirzada on behalf of respondent No.1, which are taken on record. Learned AAG files comments on behalf of respondents No.2 and 4 alongwith copies of certain documents, which are taken on record. Learned AAG submits that subject FIR has been recommended for disposal under C-class, therefore, this petition has become infructuous having served its purpose. On being confronted with the aforesaid position, learned counsel for the petitioners also seeks disposal of the petition. In view of the above, this petition has become infructuous and the same stands disposed of accordingly alongwith pending application. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar

In view of the above, this petition has become infructuous and the same stands disposed of accordingly alongwith pending application. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-1890 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on urgency application 2. For orders on office objection(s) 3. For orders on exemption application 4. For hearing of main case 23.10.2025 Mr. Shamsuddin Rajper, advocate for petitioner(s) *** 1. Urgency is granted. 2to4. Through this petition, petitioner seeks directions against the respondents No.2 and 3 to decide his pending application regarding establishment and operation of van stand at Plots No.34/B, 35/B, 37/B and 38/B, Block-C Unit No.7 Latifabad Hyderabad. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that petitioner had filed an application before Deputy Commissioner Hyderabad /respondent No.2 for grant of permission to establish and operate the van stand at aforesaid plots, which is pending since 11.06.2024. Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri Assistant A.G Sindh, present in Court in some other matters, waives the notice and states that application of the petitioner will be decided strictly in accordance with law within shortest period. Learned counsel for the petitioner is satisfied and seeks disposal of the petition accordingly. In view of the above, instant petition stands disposed of with directions to Deputy Commissioner Hyderabad/respondent No.2 to decide the application of petitioner dated 11.06.2024 within a period of 30 days from today strictly in accordance with law after providing ample opportunity of hearing to all concerned. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to Deputy Commissioner Hyderabad as well as to the office of learned Additional A.G Sindh for information and compliance.

In view of the above, instant petition stands disposed of with directions to Deputy Commissioner Hyderabad/respondent No.2 to decide the application of petitioner dated 11.06.2024 within a period of 30 days from today strictly in accordance with law after providing ample opportunity of hearing to all concerned. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to Deputy Commissioner Hyderabad as well as to the office of learned Additional A.G Sindh for information and compliance. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar
